STRUCTURAL.description: Checks that every domain has the correct SDTM-IG 3.4 column structure — required variables present, proper column ordering (STUDYID first, DOMAIN second), and correct data types.

TEMPORAL.description: Validates date logic within and across domains — reference dates are chronologically ordered, study day derivations are correct, and no temporal impossibilities exist.

TERMINOLOGY.description: Verifies that coded values (AESEV, AEOUT, SEX, RACE, etc.) use CDISC Controlled Terminology and that dictionary-derived terms (AEDECOD, CMDECOD) are populated.

COHERENCE.description: Cross-domain logical consistency — subjects who died have matching DM/DS/AE records, ARM matches ACTARM for enrolled subjects, exposure aligns with disposition.

DERIVED.description: Validates computed variables — study day (--DY) correctly derived from reference start date, EPOCH assigned from Subject Elements timeline.

FDA Submission Rules.description: Checks required by FDA for electronic submissions — derived from Pinnacle 21 Community validation rules (SD-series). Errors here would cause a Technical Rejection of the submission.

Domain Structure Rules.description: Domain-specific structural rules — SE epochs have no gaps, DS has at most one discontinuation per subject, reference ranges are populated for quantitative labs, no semantic duplicates.

Date and Temporal Integrity.description: Validates that all dates are valid ISO 8601, in logical chronological order, and consistent across domains (e.g., no events after death).

Cross-domain Consistency.description: Ensures values that appear in multiple domains are aligned — ARM matches ACTARM, STUDYID is uniform, EPOCH matches SE timeline, dictionary-derived terms are populated.

Clinical Plausibility.description: Checks that clinical values fall within physiologically possible ranges and that the clinical narrative is internally consistent (e.g., medical history pre-dates study start).

Trial Design Integrity.description: Validates consistency between trial design domains (TA, TE, TV, TS, TI) and the subject-level data — arms match between TA and DM, planned visits in TV appear in SV, elements in TE appear in SE, required TS parameters are present.
